data.title: BlackRock (BLK) Raises Dividend 10% and Announces New Stock Buybacks
data.description:
Shares of BlackRock (BLK) are up 6% on Jan. 15 after the world's largest asset manager announced that it is increasing its quarterly cash dividend by 10% and has authorized the repurchase of more stock.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Going forward, BlackRock will pay a quarterly dividend of $5.73 per share to its stockholders, a 10% increase from the previous distribution of $5.22. Additionally, the company led by CEO Larry Fink plans to buy back seven million more shares as part of its current stock repurchase program.

The shareholder returns were announced alongside strong fourth quarter 2025 financial results from BlackRock. The Wall Street firm said that its assets under management (AUM) grew 22% year-over-year in the fourth quarter to reach a record $14.04 trillion.

BlackRock's Strong Growth Trajectory

For Q4 2025, BlackRock reported a 10% increase in its earnings per share (EPS) to $13.16. Revenue rose 23% to $7.01 billion. Both the top and bottom line numbers beat Wall Street's consensus forecast that called for earnings of $12.24 a share on $6.76 billion in revenue.

The stock market boom of the past few years has led to strong growth at BlackRock, which specializes in offering investors a variety of mutual funds and exchange-traded funds (ETFs). The company said that clients added $689 billion in new assets last year, generating 9% organic fee growth for the company.

Is BLK Stock a Buy?

BlackRock's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among eight Wall Street analysts. That rating was based on six Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average BLK price target of $1,321.25 implies 14% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Investor Praises Optimus V3 Robot – 'Nobody Will Remember that Tesla Ever Made a Car'
data.description:
Angel investor Jason Calacanis recently stated that after seeing EV maker Tesla's (TSLA) Optimus V3 robot, people may one day forget that Tesla ever made cars. Speaking about a private visit to Tesla's Optimus lab, he described walking in on a Sunday morning and finding a large team actively working. He said what he saw was so impressive that it completely changed how he thinks about Tesla's future. According to Calacanis, Optimus V3 is not just another product but something that could become far bigger than Tesla's car business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, Calacanis said that Optimus will be able to use advanced AI, similar to large language models, to understand the world and carry out real-world tasks that humans do not want to do. He even suggested that there could eventually be a one-to-one ratio of robots to people, meaning one Optimus for every human. In his view, this would make Optimus the most transformative technology product ever created, which is why he thinks Tesla has already won the race in humanoid robots.

While Calacanis' remarks are clearly his personal opinion, they stand out because he is one of the first non-Tesla insiders to speak publicly about Optimus V3. At the same time, Tesla has kept tight control over what has been shown to the public. Elon Musk has said that Optimus V3 has not yet been officially revealed and that photos and videos online still show earlier versions. However, when Calacanis' comments were shared on X, Musk replied simply with "Probably true," which added even more attention to the robot's potential.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 11.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Powell's DOJ Probe is an 'Assault on the Independence of the Fed,' Warns Official
data.description:
Several global economic leaders have expressed alarm about the Department of Justice (DOJ) investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell. On Thursday, Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr added his voice to the growing list and warned that the independence of the central bank is at stake.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I think these are just examples of the ways in which the independence of the Fed is being challenged," Barr said in an interview with Yahoo Finance.

Barr Reaffirms Fed's Focus on Inflation and Jobs

Barr emphasized that the Fed's interest rate decisions are based solely on its dual mandate of price stability and maximizing employment, keeping politics out of the process.

Earlier this week, Powell disclosed that the DOJ had launched an investigation into his oversight of the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. He claimed the probe served as a pretext for the Fed setting policy independently instead of adhering to President Trump's demands for lower rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Dell Technologies Stock Rises as Top Barclays Analyst Upgrades to 'Buy'
data.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) stock was up about 2% on Thursday, at the time of writing, as Barclays analyst Tim Long upgraded the PC and server company to Buy from Hold with a price target of $148. The 5-star analyst is now more positive on DELL stock, given the strength in the company's artificial intelligence (AI) server orders and several other positives.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Barclays Analyst Turns Bullish on DELL Stock

Aside from robust AI server orders, Long also noted the stability in Dell's AI operating margins, opportunities in enterprise server and storage, and the company's efficiency in managing operating expenses.

Long noted that Dell plans to ship about $9.4 billion of AI servers in Q4, bringing the full-year shipments to $25 billion. The 5-star analyst expects 155% and 60% growth in AI servers in Fiscal 2026 and Fiscal 2027.

The analyst also noted recovery in Dell's enterprise server and storage end markets. Long added that Dell continues to increase the mix of its IP offerings in storage. Meanwhile, for traditional servers, the analyst noted that the company still has 70% of its installed base consisting of older-generation products, indicating substantial upgrade opportunities. Long also believes in DELL's ability to sail through the current commodity backdrop, given its supply chain leadership.

Is DELL Stock a Good Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Dell Technologies stock based on 13 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average DELL stock price target of $164.86 indicates about 36% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Ondas (ONDS) Stock Wins a New Street-High Price Target
data.description:
Ondas (NASDAQ:ONDS) stock has surged by 500% over the past year, which is a remarkable return anyway you look at it. But are those gains based on the drone maker's real-world potential, or is this name just the latest to benefit from a retail-driven frenzy?

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stifel analyst Jonathan Siegmann says that the latter notion is one he hears frequently, specifically that ONDS is "just a hyped-up retail stock," and that he does not know "how to do math" with the stock trading at 175x revenues.

However, Siegmann argues that a $1 billion investment from a single institutional investor "runs directly counter to that view."

What the analyst is referring to is a $1 billion registered direct offering with an institutional investor announced last Friday. The offering consists of 19 million shares, along with pre-funded warrants covering up to 41.8 million additional shares, all priced at $16.45 per share. The warrants are exercisable at $28 per share and have a seven-year term. If fully exercised, they could provide ONDS with up to $3.4 billion in incremental capital. The company plans to deploy the net proceeds toward corporate development and long-term strategic growth initiatives, including acquisitions, joint ventures, and investments.

This $1 billion capital raise comes on the heels of the $850 million raised in the second half of 2025. Siegmann thinks the expanded capital base reinforces ONDS' position as a credible, long-term DoW supplier, while also strengthening its appeal as a buyer to "cash-starved start-ups," an employer for industry leaders seeking to join an "industry winner," and a partner for third-party contract manufacturers looking to gain exposure to the sector.

"We think there will be case studies written on this differentiated approach in 2035 and investors should be doing diligence on ONDS now," the analyst further said.

ONDS is hosting a virtual investor day tomorrow (January 16th) where Siegmann thinks management will provide additional detail on recent announcements, order activity, and its M&A outlook for 2026, supported by newly raised capital ready for deployment.

Separately, on January 8th, Andreessen Horowitz announced a $15 billion fundraise, with approximately $1.2 billion earmarked for its "American Dynamism" strategy, which includes investments in national security and defense. "The deep pockets of private capital are already all-in on this niche and we think the market's vote of confidence for ONDS arms them with the capital firepower to compete," Siegmann added.

The analyst also thinks ONDS remains "one of the most misunderstood names" he covers and urges clients to become more familiar with its differentiated operating model.

To this end, Siegmann rates ONDS shares a Buy, while raising his price target to a Street-high of $17, suggesting the stock will surge 27% in the months ahead. (To watch Siegmann's track record, click here)

All in all, this stock offers something of a conundrum on Wall Street right now; on the one hand, based on a unanimous 8 Buys, the analyst consensus rates the shares a Strong Buy. However, the $11.5 average target points toward a 12-month decline of 10%. Given the discrepancy, keep an eye out for either further price target hikes or rating downgrades. (See ONDS stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: "A License to Print Money": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up as Lithium Refinery Goes Live
data.description:
It is no secret that electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) requires lithium to live. Lithium is a key component in electric vehicle batteries, and requires a sort of refined lithium that is difficult to come by. But Tesla is currently refining lithium on a large scale, answering its own supply chain question and potentially opening up a new revenue stream. The news was a mild help to shareholders, who sent Tesla shares up fractionally in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla CEO Elon Musk refers to lithium refining as "…a license to print money." And this is reasonable enough; lithium ore by itself is not vanishingly rare. The problem is that you cannot simply plug a rock into a battery and make it store power. The lithium has to be refined, and this process is actually fairly involved. Thus, refined lithium is often difficult to get and increases prices.

Enter Tesla's lithium refinery in Robstown, Texas, not far from Corpus Christi. The process to get this refinery off the ground was surprisingly swift, going from breaking ground in 2023 to launching a full, integrated startup in the closing days of 2025. Now, the plant is up and running, and represents the "…first spodumene to lithium hydroxide refinery in North America." Better yet, the plant runs without acid, which represents a major potential safety hazard gone.

Something Needs to Happen

While Elon Musk has spent a lot of time promising the world at Tesla, he has recently pulled back on a lot of it, and at a time when Deutsche Bank analysts are calling for Tesla to ramp things up. In fact, Deutsche Bank analysts specifically pointed out that a lot of Tesla's biggest moneymakers are comparatively smaller in scope right now.

A note from Deutsche Bank mentioned, "While the autos business at Tesla may underperform in 2026, we think more attention is directed towards the company's robotaxi expansion and efforts at humanoid development." It also looked for "…the race for autonomy and embodied AI" to ramp up significantly in 2026.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 6.13% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 12.74% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Climbs on Upbeat Jobs Data and Tech Strength
data.description:
After opening in the red, the Dow Jones (DJIA) staged a recovery and is now trading on higher ground, driven by tech stocks and a positive labor market update.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Initial jobless claims, which serves as a gauge of layoffs, fell by 9,000 to 198,000 for the week ended January 9 and were below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Furthermore, continuing jobless claims, which track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits, decreased by 19,000 to 1.884 million and undercut the estimate of 1.897 million. Continuing claims trail initial claims by one week.

Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said he wasn't surprised by the claims data, adding that the Fed's main priority at the moment is bringing inflation down to 2%. He also warned that a loss of Fed independence amplifies the risk of inflation amid a Justice Department probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell. "You're going to get inflation come roaring back if you try to take away the independence of the central bank," Goolsbee told CNBC's "Squawk Box."

Elsewhere, geopolitical tensions took a step down after Iran's foreign minister said the country had "no plan" to execute protesters. President Trump acknowledged the news but didn't rule out military action against Iran.

At the same time, tensions on the home front intensified after Trump said he was considering invoking the Insurrection Act to address protests in Minnesota. The federal law allows the president to deploy the U.S. military domestically to help suppress unrest, insurrection, or rebellion. Protests in Minneapolis have ramped up in recent days as residents push back against the influx of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.82% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why is the Stock Market Up Today, 1/15/2026?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Thursday following an encouraging labor market update. In addition, AI-related stocks, including Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO), are rising in sympathy on the back of an optimistic earnings report from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM).

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Initial jobless claims, which serve as a gauge of layoffs, decreased by 9,000 to 198,000 for the week ended January 9. Furthermore, continuing jobless claims, which reflect ongoing unemployment, fell by 19,000 to 1.884 million.

Trump Says Killings in Iran Have Stopped

Easing geopolitical tensions are also pushing the market higher today. On Wednesday evening, President Trump said the "killing in Iran is stopping," and that the country had halted plans to execute protesters.

Earlier this week, Trump told Iranian protesters that "HELP IS ON ITS WAY," without specifying any clear action. It remains uncertain if Trump will involve the U.S. military in Iran, although tensions have cooled with global diplomatic pressure contributing to a temporary de-escalation.

data.title: Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Surges as Employee Fired Over Cup Incident
data.description:
Three days ago, coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) found itself embroiled in a new incident involving a cup doodle that proved too awkward to countenance. A barista decided to draw a pig on the side of a cup that went to a sheriff's deputy in California, prompting outrage from the law enforcement community. Starbucks stepped in, and now a barista is looking for work. This appears to have appeased investors just as much, as Starbucks shares gained over 2% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starbucks launched an internal investigation shortly after the story came to light, and discovered that the incident was not so much malicious as it was accidental. The drawing had been placed on the cup well before the officer showed up, and was inadvertently used to fill the officer's order. Starbucks noted that the cup was meant to "…uplift partners working behind the bar, not to be given to a customer." The pig in question is part of a meme known as "John Pork," described as a "…virtual influencer and self-proclaimed fashion model" from Palermo, Italy.

Starbucks further noted that it moved to "…address the situation immediately inside the store and make it right," reports noted. Starbucks reps said, "This was unacceptable. We have reached out to the customer several times and leaders in the sheriff's department to apologize."

Then The Other Lawsuit Hit

This by itself would be difficult for a business to deal with, but it got worse for Starbucks from there. A law firm in Seattle filed suit against Starbucks over its coffee supply chain and the chemicals used in making decaffeinated coffee. Hagens Berman filed the suit, a class-action in the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington.

The suit alleges that while Starbucks may call its coffee farms "ethical," the actual practices are violations of labor law. Though it is worth wondering whose labor laws are being violated, and what ramifications this has on Hagens Berman's case. Further, the Decaf House Blend in Starbucks has been found to contain toluene, benzene, and methylene chloride. All of these are dangerous, in certain quantities. Starbucks, for its part, believes the allegations are "…inaccurate and misrepresent both our sourcing practices and the integrity of our Coffee and Farmer Equity (C.A.F.E.) Practices program."

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 12 Buys, seven Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 3.47% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $96.73 per share implies 3.63% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
